description: Lock the canonical GPU node to the 1999 GeForce 256/NV10-era integrated graphics processor scope and prevent the removed duplicate GPU-parallel-processors prerequisite from returning.

checks.reason: The canonical node is scoped to the modern single-chip GPU class popularized by the 1999 GeForce 256/NV10, not to 1958 computer graphics generally.
AI accelerators should use the canonical GPU node as the GPU lineage/enabling prerequisite.
The corrected AI accelerator GPU edge is enabling because GPUs are one accelerator platform, not a hard prerequisite for every AI accelerator.
The duplicate gpu_parallel_processors node was removed and must not be reintroduced as the AI accelerator prerequisite.
3D computer graphics demand enabled GPU development but should not be modeled as a hard prerequisite for every GPU-like parallel processor.
Broad deep learning is anchored to 1986 representation-learning/backpropagation work and must not depend on a 1999 GPU node.
The broad CNN lineage is anchored to Fukushima's 1980 neocognitron rather than the 2012 GPU-trained AlexNet milestone.
Broad CNN architecture predates the modern GPU class; GPUs are later scaling accelerators for specific CNN systems.
Digital photography sensors are a data-source/application context, not a prerequisite for the CNN architecture.
